mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across a 5-year out-of-sample walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE is below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based domestic demand expansion, which sustains corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase MoM
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series declines, or Rank IC drops below 0.04 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term positive S&P 500 returns as markets price in improved operational efficiency and output growth.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns do not materialize during tightening phases when INDPRO rises, or the signal reverses direction when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading above 2.5% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in > 70% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 255.0, or the signal shows no predictive power when inflation is below 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects sustained labor market strength and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS changes remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no directional bias during payroll expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales signals demand fragmentation, where weak consumer spending offsets broader economic output, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns, or positive returns occur in > 60% of triggered instances during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates rapid supply chain normalization and capacity utilization, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns as markets price in operational rebound.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during recovery phases, or the signal loses statistical significance when tested against a baseline of zero returns.
